unit04.title: Unit 4 - The Design Process
unit04.summary: Students shift from solving their own problems to designing with empathy for others. They practice user-centered design, paper prototyping, feedback cycles, and build an App Lab prototype as a team.
unit04.description: The Design Process unit transitions students toward considering the broader social impacts of computing. Through a series of design challenges, students empathize with users, identify needs, and iterate on solutions. The second half of the unit centers on a team project: students define a need they care about, prototype solutions on paper and in App Lab, and test with real users to drive further iteration.

chapters.description: User Centered Design: empathize with users, define and prepare solutions, try and reflect with feedback, and learn to use paper prototypes to test ideas quickly.
chapters.goal01: Empathize with users to identify needs and opportunities.
chapters.goal02: Apply a user-centered design cycle: Define, Prepare, Try, Reflect.
chapters.goal03: Gather, categorize, and act on feedback to improve designs.
chapters.goal04: Use paper prototypes to test assumptions before coding.
chapters.goal05: Connect UI choices to user needs and usability outcomes.
chapters.big questions 01: How can we choose and design products based on the needs of a user?
chapters.big questions 02: How do we plan, test, and refine ideas efficiently before we code?
chapters.big questions 03: What makes a user interface effective for its intended audience?

lessons.title: Designing With Empathy
lessons.overview: Investigate the design of non-technical objects (shoes) to match products to users and practice the “I Like / I Wish / I Wonder” feedback structure.
lessons.question: How can we choose products based on the needs of a user?
lessons.content: Analyze example products, match them to users, and propose changes to improve usefulness and usability.
lessons.objective1: Identify likely users and needs from product features.
lessons.objective2: Provide structured feedback using I Like / I Wish / I Wonder.
lessons.objective3: Propose user-centered improvements to a design.

lessons.title: Understanding Your User
lessons.overview: Role‑play with user profiles to empathize with potential users, select appropriate products, critique designs, and suggest improvements.
lessons.question: How can we make sure a product is meeting the needs of a user?
lessons.content: Adopt a user profile, react to products as that user, and recommend design changes.
lessons.objective1: Explain how empathy informs design choices.
lessons.objective2: Infer needs and barriers from a user profile.
lessons.objective3: Recommend improvements aligned to user needs.

lessons.title: User Centered Design – Define and Prepare
lessons.overview: Begin a two‑day mini‑project: brainstorm users of smart clothing, group and select needs, and choose a focus using the Define and Prepare steps.
lessons.question: How can we design for people other than ourselves?
lessons.content: Brainstorm users and needs; categorize and choose a need to address; plan a solution approach.
lessons.objective1: Generate and categorize user needs for a problem space.
lessons.objective2: Select a target need using criteria and constraints.
lessons.objective3: Plan next steps for prototyping based on user needs.

lessons.title: User Centered Design – Try and Reflect
lessons.overview: Complete the mini‑project by designing smart clothing that addresses the chosen need; share decision‑making and gather feedback.
lessons.question: How can we design a product to meet a user's needs?
lessons.content: Build and present a concept addressing the selected need; reflect using feedback to guide iteration.
lessons.objective1: Prototype a solution guided by user needs and constraints.
lessons.objective2: Communicate rationale for design decisions.
lessons.objective3: Use feedback to reflect and plan improvements.

lessons.title: User Interfaces
lessons.overview: Use a paper prototype of a sample chat app to see how low‑fidelity prototypes surface issues and support rapid testing and feedback.
lessons.question: How can we test an app to make sure it meets a user's needs?
lessons.content: Interact with a provided paper prototype and identify improvements for the next iteration.
lessons.objective1: Explain the purpose and value of paper prototyping.
lessons.objective2: Identify UI issues and opportunities from a paper prototype.
lessons.objective3: Prioritize improvements for the next iteration.

lessons.title: Feedback and Testing
lessons.overview: Categorize feedback from paper‑prototype “users” and implement UI changes that address identified needs.
lessons.question: How can we use feedback to improve an app?
lessons.content: Sort user feedback, translate needs into UI changes, and update the prototype.
lessons.objective1: Organize feedback to reveal patterns and priorities.
lessons.objective2: Translate user needs into actionable UI changes.
lessons.objective3: Implement and document changes based on feedback.

chapters.description: App Prototyping: interview and identify user needs, create paper prototypes, plan a socially impactful app, build and link screens in App Lab, and iterate through testing to a final presentation.
chapters.goal01: Plan and scope an app concept grounded in real user needs.
chapters.goal02: Prototype on paper and in App Lab; link screens and handle events.
chapters.goal03: Test with real users and iterate to address bugs and features.
chapters.goal04: Research existing apps and UI elements to inform design.
chapters.goal05: Communicate design process and outcomes in a final presentation.
chapters.big questions 01: How can interviews, research, and testing guide app design?
chapters.big questions 02: What App Lab features and UI elements best support our goals?
chapters.big questions 03: How do we plan, build, test, and present as a design team?

lessons.title: Identifying User Needs
lessons.overview: Extract needs and interests from user interviews to motivate an app idea addressing barriers or opportunities.
lessons.question: How can user interviews help us create apps to meet the needs of a user?
lessons.content: Interpret interviews; identify barriers/opportunities; brainstorm app ideas; select a direction.
lessons.objective1: Analyze interview data to derive user needs.
lessons.objective2: Brainstorm app concepts that address needs.
lessons.objective3: Decide on an app direction as a team.

lessons.title: Project – Paper Prototype
lessons.overview: Create individual paper prototypes based on interviews; test with users and refine assumptions about UI and flow.
lessons.question: How can I develop an app prototype for a user?
lessons.content: Draft screens on cards, run user tests, and document feedback for iteration.
lessons.objective1: Produce a paper prototype that maps tasks and screens.
lessons.objective2: Test with users and capture actionable feedback.
lessons.objective3: Revise the prototype based on test results.

lessons.title: Designing Apps for Good
lessons.overview: Form project teams, set team norms, and explore student‑built ‘apps for good’ examples to choose a topic, users, and vision.
lessons.question: What is an App for Good we want to create?
lessons.content: Create a team contract; explore examples; agree on topic, users, and goals.
lessons.objective1: Establish team roles and collaboration norms.
lessons.objective2: Identify a socially impactful app focus.
lessons.objective3: Articulate target users and success criteria.

lessons.title: Market Research
lessons.overview: Research comparable apps to refine user needs, scope features, and maintain citations for final presentation.
lessons.question: How can we use existing apps as inspiration for our own apps?
lessons.content: Search, evaluate, and cite related apps; refine target users and features.
lessons.objective1: Apply credibility criteria to evaluate apps.
lessons.objective2: Extract feature ideas aligned to user needs.
lessons.objective3: Maintain a citations list for sources.

lessons.title: Exploring UI Elements
lessons.overview: Study App Lab example apps to identify UI components and map them to features in your team’s design.
lessons.question: What user interface elements will be useful for our app?
lessons.content: Explore example apps; list UI components; plan component usage in your app.
lessons.objective1: Identify App Lab UI components and their purposes.
lessons.objective2: Align desired features to feasible components.
lessons.objective3: Draft a component map for planned screens.

lessons.title: Build a Paper Prototype
lessons.overview: Create team paper prototypes with page plans, screen assignments, and coordinated flow.
lessons.question: How can we build a paper prototype for our app?
lessons.content: Sketch each screen; assign work across the team; assemble a coherent prototype.
lessons.objective1: Produce a multi‑screen paper prototype as a team.
lessons.objective2: Coordinate screen responsibilities and conventions.
lessons.objective3: Ensure flow and navigation are consistent across screens.

lessons.title: Prototype Testing
lessons.overview: Plan and conduct paper‑prototype tests with users, assigning roles and gathering observations and post‑test responses.
lessons.question: How does our prototype work when tested by real users?
lessons.content: Assign roles (narrator/computer/observers); test with users; capture findings.
lessons.objective1: Develop a clear test plan and scenarios.
lessons.objective2: Observe user behavior and collect feedback.
lessons.objective3: Synthesize findings to guide revisions.

lessons.title: Design Mode in App Lab
lessons.overview: Learn App Lab design mode by building an exemplar screen, then plan how to adapt paper‑prototype screens with unique IDs.
lessons.question: How can we use App Lab and Design Mode to create apps?
lessons.content: Complete skill‑building levels; plan the transition from paper to digital screens.
lessons.objective1: Build UI screens in App Lab’s design mode.
lessons.objective2: Assign unique IDs and plan consistent design conventions.
lessons.objective3: Prepare a screen from the paper prototype for digital build‑out.

lessons.title: Build a Digital Prototype
lessons.overview: Individually implement at least one page of the team app in App Lab based on the paper prototype, following a guided progression.
lessons.question: How can I design a digital prototype from a paper prototype?
lessons.content: Complete skill‑building levels; design one of your app’s screens digitally.
lessons.objective1: Translate paper screens into App Lab UI.
lessons.objective2: Apply consistent styling and structure across screens.
lessons.objective3: Document design choices for team integration.

lessons.title: Events in App Lab
lessons.overview: Add screens, import teammates’ screens, and write event‑driven code to navigate between screens.
lessons.question: How can I use events to create an app?
lessons.content: Add screens and write onEvent handlers to link navigation and interactions.
lessons.objective1: Use event‑driven programming to connect UI elements.
lessons.objective2: Import and organize multiple screens in one project.
lessons.objective3: Plan team conventions for linking and navigation.

lessons.title: Linking Prototype Screens
lessons.overview: Combine team screens into a single project and link them with code to complete the digital prototype.
lessons.question: How can I combine screens to create a complete digital prototype?
lessons.content: Share import URLs; import teammates’ screens; add events to link screens.
lessons.objective1: Integrate screens from multiple projects.
lessons.objective2: Link screens and navigation coherently.
lessons.objective3: Validate flow against the design plan.

lessons.title: Testing the App
lessons.overview: Test the digital prototype with users; focus on observing behavior and gathering think‑aloud feedback to plan revisions.
lessons.question: How does our digital prototype work when tested by real users?
lessons.content: Plan scenarios; test with target users; collect observations and feedback.
lessons.objective1: Conduct structured usability tests of the digital prototype.
lessons.objective2: Record and prioritize issues and opportunities.
lessons.objective3: Plan revisions based on test insights.

lessons.title: Bugs and Features
lessons.overview: Plan improvements based on test results, addressing bugs, polishing UI, and adding small features as time allows.
lessons.question: How can we create a plan to address bugs and features in our prototype?
lessons.content: Create an improvement plan and begin implementing prioritized changes.
lessons.objective1: Differentiate bug fixes from feature updates.
lessons.objective2: Prioritize work using a shared task board.
lessons.objective3: Implement improvements individually and as a team.

lessons.title: Updating Your Prototype
lessons.overview: Track and implement updates using a To Do / Doing / Done workflow, then recombine into a single app if needed.
lessons.question: How can I track my work while we make updates to our app?
lessons.content: Execute the improvement plan and integrate updated screens.
lessons.objective1: Use a lightweight workflow to manage updates.
lessons.objective2: Integrate changes and resolve inconsistencies.
lessons.objective3: Prepare the prototype for presentation.

lessons.title: Project – App Presentation
lessons.overview: Prepare and deliver a presentation that explains the problem, the team’s design process, testing, and plans for future work.
lessons.question: How can I present the steps in our design process to people outside of our team?
lessons.content: Use the provided template to compile research, prototypes, tests, and iterations into a coherent presentation.
lessons.objective1: Communicate the design process and outcomes clearly.
lessons.objective2: Cite sources and user feedback appropriately.
lessons.objective3: Reflect on teamwork and next steps.
